summary: Bootstrapped the AI memory system from a repository scan, recorded the initial full-test baseline (`mvn -B -ntp test` passed with 398 tests), and flagged the first known process-doc drift around benchmark jar examples and SQL-like capability wording.
Reworked `/ai` into the hot/cold memory model, moved freshness tracking to `ai/memory-state.json`, and regenerated the derived indexes around the new layout.

Fixed the known process-document drift in `CONTRIBUTING.md`, `MIGRATION.md`, and `RELEASE.md`, and extended the doc-consistency scripts to check the benchmark-version and SQL-like-capability invariants.

Landed the selective single-join redesign that started in WP15/WP16: reduced `QueryField` cloning, replaced post-join schema rescans with derived metadata, introduced `FastArrayQuerySupport`, added flattened array-row projection support in `ReflectionUtil`, and kept the full test suite green while the warmed 10k computed-field join path dropped to about 1.091 ms/op.

Captured the first full documented benchmark sweep across core, chart, baseline, cache, hotspot, and legacy suites. Core and chart threshold checks passed, chart parity moved to diagnostic-only status, and the broader portfolio stress shifted attention toward absolute SQL-like/chart cost plus recurring `ReflectionUtil` and `FastArrayQuerySupport` hotspot clusters.

Parked WP17 as good enough after the accepted fast-path gains: specialized single-rule matching, narrowed reflection reads, reduced join-build allocation, and bound numeric-expression array access. The warmed 10k computed-field join path improved to about 0.614 ms/op, allocation stayed around 2.308 MB/op, and the remaining join-path hotspots centered in `ReflectionUtil` plus `FastArrayQuerySupport`.

Started WP18 by caching prepared non-subquery SQL-like execution shapes and removing the typed-projection round-trip from chart execution. Focused tests and full `mvn -q test` passed, and exact targeted 10k reruns showed SQL-like stats/query and chart paths roughly at parity with fluent controls (`4.728/4.891 ms/op` for SQL-like versus `4.990/5.045 ms/op` for fluent).

Removed the chart JSON export hotspot by rewriting `ChartPayloadJsonExporter` to pre-size buffers and write fixed-scale numeric values directly. The full suite passed, chart thresholds still passed `45/45`, and comparable cold `scatterPayloadJsonExport` timings fell to about `0.066`, `0.634`, and `1.146 ms/op` at sizes `1000`, `10000`, and `100000`.

Extended WP18 with setup-side and fast-stats follow-up work: precomputed raw execution-plan keys, removed a duplicate `QuerySpec` copy, added `preparedExecutionView(...)`, routed simple bean-backed stats through direct aggregation and indexed array-row chart mapping, cached flat read plans, and reused one SQL-like execution run per call. The broader raw-row delegation experiment regressed and was not kept; the remaining likely bottleneck stayed in SQL-like setup/query cost rather than chart assembly.

Fixed alias-safe SQL-like fast-stats projection and chart execution by remapping array-row schemas through `SqlLikeExecutionSupport` and carrying the projection class through chart fallback paths. Focused regressions plus full `mvn -q test` passed, and the latest short rerun kept SQL-like chart below SQL-like query (`4.764 ms/op` vs `6.735 ms/op`), reinforcing that the unresolved WP18 cost is now more likely setup/query work.

Compacted `/ai` under `MAINTENANCE.md`: trimmed hot state to startup-critical facts, removed stale doc-index entries, regenerated all five derived indexes from the current tree, collapsed repetitive event history into milestone summaries, and refreshed `ai/memory-state.json` against the current commit.

Added dedicated prepared SQL-like stats setup microbenchmarks to `HotspotMicroJmhBenchmark`, wired them into the hotspot suite and docs, and verified with `mvn -q test` plus a forked `size=10000`, `-f 1 -wi 2 -i 5 -r 100ms -prof gc` run. Rebind view measured about `0.324 us/op` / `3,120 B/op` versus `0.668 us/op` / `3,528 B/op` for copy, while full rebind-plus-fast-stats setup stayed around `7.517 ms/op` / `16,785,798 B/op` for view versus `7.306 ms/op` / `16,786,181 B/op` for copy, indicating row-scan/aggregation now dominates that path.

Updated `TODO.md` with explicit consolidation guidance after reviewing active hot paths: keep `WP18`/`WP19` prioritized, share setup/metadata plans first, and avoid merging specialized bean, `QueryRow`, and `Object[]` execution loops just to remove duplication. The review called out `FilterImpl.filterRows(...)` vs `SqlLikeQuery.executeRawRows(...)` as later consolidation work only, because a broader shared raw-row path had already regressed the benchmark.

Landed a WP18 fast-stats pass that replaced per-row bucket `String.format(...)` work in `TimeBucketUtil`, short-circuited string group-key conversion, and specialized the common single-group fast-stats aggregation path. Focused regressions plus full `mvn -q test` passed with `433` tests, the rebuilt prepared-stats setup microbenchmark dropped to about `509.906 us/op` / `2,145,675 B/op` for copy and `512.749 us/op` / `2,145,195 B/op` for view at `size=10000`, and exact targeted whole-query reruns on the bean-backed month-bucket stats workload measured about `0.529`, `0.531`, `0.519`, and `0.526 ms/op` for fluent query, fluent chart, SQL-like query, and SQL-like chart respectively.

Extended the 2026-03-16 WP18 validation sweep after the fast-stats pass. Exact targeted reruns showed grouped stats query/chart at about `0.273`, `0.273`, `0.277`, and `0.264 ms/op` and multi-series line/area mapping at about `0.566`, `0.575`, `0.577`, and `0.572 ms/op` for fluent/query-like variants at `size=10000`. A rebuilt full chart guardrail suite still passed `45/45`, and the clearest remaining larger chart workload became scatter mapping (`2.924 ms/op` fluent / `4.613 ms/op` SQL-like at `size=10000`, `19.743` / `24.991 ms/op` at `size=100000`).

Shifted active work from WP18 to WP19 after the 2026-03-16 stats/chart checkpoint. A narrow `ReflectionUtil` projection-cast fast path plus a rebuilt hotspot-suite rerun (`target/benchmarks/hotspots-gc-2026-03-16.json`) measured `reflectionToClassList|size=10000` at about `852.025 us/op` / `1,400,236 B/op` and `reflectionToDomainRows|size=10000` at about `418.191 us/op` / `2,840,026 B/op`, materially lower in latency than the recorded 2026-03-14 snapshot while allocation stayed effectively flat. WP19 remains open pending a refreshed warmed JFR on the current tree.

Refreshed the warmed WP19 profile on the current tree with `PojoLensJoinJmhBenchmark.pojoLensJoinLeftComputedField -p size=10000 -f 1 -wi 5 -i 10 -r 300ms -jvmArgsAppend "-XX:StartFlightRecording=filename=target/wp19-current-2026-03-16.jfr,settings=profile,dumponexit=true"`. The run measured about `0.666 ms/op` with JFR overhead and kept the same dominant class cluster: `ReflectionUtil$ResolvedFieldPath.read` (`837` CPU / `4220` alloc first-repo-frame samples) plus `FastArrayQuerySupport.applyComputedValues`, `tryBuildJoinedState`, `buildChildIndex`, and `materializeJoinedRow`. Two smaller follow-up optimizations were benchmark-flat and not kept, so the next WP19 target should move toward joined-row materialization/indexing or a more structural reflection-read reuse change.

Ran the agreed hard-stop structural WP19 spike on `FastArrayQuerySupport.tryBuildJoinedState()` by prefiltering joined rows before storing the fast state. On the actual warmed target (`PojoLensJoinJmhBenchmark.pojoLensJoinLeftComputedField|size=10000`, `-f 1 -wi 5 -i 10 -r 300ms`) it regressed from about `0.584 ms/op` to about `0.700 ms/op`, so it was reverted immediately. A clean rerun on the reverted code recovered to about `0.595 ms/op`, and WP19 was parked pending a materially different structural hypothesis.

Completed WP20 computed-field rebaselining. Repeated strict-style cold reruns of `PojoLensJoinJmhBenchmark.pojoLensJoinLeftComputedField` measured about `2.597`/`3.766`/`6.020 ms/op` at `size=1000` and `114.241`/`113.818`/`60.348 ms/op` at `size=10000`; warmed `-prof gc` reruns measured PojoLens about `0.063`/`0.603 ms/op` with `247,520`/`2,302,715 B/op` versus manual about `0.009`/`0.098 ms/op` with `84,512`/`927,128 B/op`. The core budgets were tightened from `250/500 ms/op` to `25/200 ms/op`, the hotspot remained diagnostic-only at about `319.695 us/op` / `3,532,314 B/op`, and a rebuilt strict core suite still passed `42/42`.

Landed a focused WP18 scatter-mapping pass in `ChartMapper` after fresh warmed scatter reruns measured about `1.476`/`13.848 ms/op` for fluent and `1.536`/`14.639 ms/op` for SQL-like at `size=10000/100000`. The mapper now accumulates multi-series charts through dense label/series indexes and caches validated x-axis labels once per distinct x instead of building nested per-series maps keyed by repeated formatted x strings. Matching warmed reruns improved to about `1.321`/`9.939 ms/op` for fluent and `1.300`/`9.760 ms/op` for SQL-like, with `size=100000` allocation dropping from about `41.3/38.5 MB/op` to `36.6/33.8 MB/op`. Focused regressions plus full `mvn -q test` passed with `436` tests, and a rebuilt full chart guardrail rerun still passed `45/45`.

Tried a second WP19 structural spike by deferring fast-array joined-row materialization and filtering directly to compact projected rows on the no-order path. Comparable short `-prof gc` reruns of `PojoLensJoinJmhBenchmark.(pojoLensJoinLeftComputedField|manualHashJoinLeftComputedField)` measured PojoLens at about `0.073 ms/op` / `220,040 B/op` for `size=1000` and `0.712 ms/op` / `2,022,740 B/op` for `size=10000`, versus the current warmed checkpoint of about `0.063 ms/op` / `247,520 B/op` and `0.603 ms/op` / `2,302,715 B/op`. Allocation improved but absolute warmed latency regressed, so the spike was reverted. The reverted tree then passed `mvn -q test` again with `436` tests.

Started the post-WP18/WP19 consolidation track with a cold-path helper pass. Added `CollectionUtil` to centralize repeated `firstNonNull(...)` and generic limit-copy behavior, then switched `FilterQueryBuilder`, `FastArrayQuerySupport`, `FastStatsQuerySupport`, `FilterImpl`, `ChartMapper`, `SqlLikeQuery`, and `SqlLikeExecutionSupport` over to it. Added `CollectionUtilTest`, and the full suite passed again with `441` tests.

Continued the consolidation track with a shared row-adapter pass. Added `QueryRowAdapterSupport` to centralize the duplicated cold-path `Object[] -> QueryRow` materializer previously repeated in `FastArrayQuerySupport` and `FastStatsQuerySupport`, added `QueryRowAdapterSupportTest`, and reran the full suite successfully with `443` tests.

Continued the consolidation track with a shared capacity-helper pass. `CollectionUtil` now also owns the repeated map-capacity sizing rule through `expectedMapCapacity(...)`, and `AggregationEngine`, `FilterCore`, `GroupEngine`, `JoinEngine`, `FastArrayQuerySupport`, and `FastStatsQuerySupport` now use it instead of local copies. The full suite passed again with `444` tests.

Continued the consolidation track with a shared exact-name schema/index helper. Added `SchemaIndexUtil` to centralize exact-name field-index planning for both raw field-name schemas and `QueryRow` field lists, switched `ChartMapper` and `SqlLikeExecutionSupport` to it, added `SchemaIndexUtilTest`, and reran focused regressions plus the full suite successfully with `446` tests. `scripts/check-doc-consistency.ps1` also passed.

Continued the consolidation track with a shared exact-name `QueryField` lookup helper. Added `QueryFieldLookupUtil` to centralize `QueryField` index and value lookup, switched `ChartMapper`, `SqlLikeExecutionSupport`, `JoinEngine`, and `RuleCleaner` to it, added `QueryFieldLookupUtilTest`, and reran focused regressions plus the full suite successfully with `448` tests. `scripts/check-doc-consistency.ps1` also passed.

Cleared `TODO.md` by request and updated hot-session memory so `current-state.md` and `handoff.md` no longer treat it as the active backlog. The old consolidation guidance was preserved directly in AI state.

Continued the consolidation track with a shared schema-name helper follow-up. Extended `SchemaIndexUtil` to centralize ordered schema-name extraction for `QueryField` lists and first-usable `QueryRow` rows, switched `FilterExecutionPlan` and `ReflectionUtil` to it, added coverage in `SchemaIndexUtilTest` and `ReflectionUtilTest`, and reran focused regressions plus the full suite successfully with `451` tests.

Continued the consolidation track with a shared ordered-entry helper follow-up. Added `CollectionUtil.sortedEntriesByKey(...)`, switched `FilterExecutionPlan`, `FilterExecutionPlanCacheKey`, and `TabularSchemaSupport` to it, added two more `CollectionUtilTest` cases, and reran focused regressions plus the full suite successfully with `453` tests.

Checked the repository's Java lint flow. `mvn -B -ntp -Plint verify -DskipTests` still produced `target/checkstyle-result.xml`, but `scripts/check-lint-baseline.ps1` reported `11,214` current checkstyle entries versus the `5,770`-entry baseline, with `7,424` marked new and `1,980` baseline entries already resolved, so the committed lint baseline is materially stale relative to the current tree.
